theory.intro: To draw an angle of a given size, use a protractor to mark the second arm at the correct degree.
facts: Start with a base arm, then place the protractor centre on its endpoint with $0^{\circ}$ along the arm.
Mark a dot at the required degree, then rule the second arm through it.
For reflex angles ($> 180^{\circ}$), draw the inner angle $360^{\circ} -$ (reflex) and mark the outside.
steps: Draw a base arm and place the protractor centre on its end.
Find the required degree and mark a point.
Join the vertex to the point with a ruler.
